case.paper_id: Unique ID for the paper/case, e.g., Bruner_ExpEco_2017_amYY.
case.study_type: Study type, e.g., experimental, observational, survey, meta-analysis, other.
case.focal_claim: The focal claim being tested for analytical robustness.
case.case_mode: Execution setting for this case, e.g., reference_guided or agent_driven.

original_datasets.name: Name of the original dataset used for robustness reanalysis.
original_datasets.file_format: File format, e.g., csv, dta, rdata, rds, xlsx, json, parquet.
original_datasets.columns: List of key variable names, if available.

summary_statistics.info: Structural metadata such as dimensions, column types, or equivalent.
summary_statistics.describe: Descriptive statistics such as mean, std, min, max, where applicable. If not available, return null.

access.url: Download or access URL if available.
access.restrictions: License or usage restrictions, if any.

original_datasets.notes: Any caveats about the original data, such as missing metadata, unclear variable names, or required preprocessing.

planned_method.planned_id: Unique ID for this analysis plan, e.g., paperID_analysisID.

analysis_path.path_name: Short name for the analysis path.
analysis_path.path_description: Concise description of the analysis path to execute.
analysis_path.software: Software or language used, e.g., R, Stata, Matlab, Python.
analysis_path.model_family: Main model or test family, e.g., linear_regression, logistic_regression, correlation, t_test, poisson, multilevel, Bayesian, or null if unclear.
preprocessing_steps: Step-by-step description of preprocessing and analysis preparation.

key_choices.outcome_measure: How the outcome variable is measured or constructed.
key_choices.main_predictor_measure: How the main predictor is measured or constructed.
key_choices.sample_restriction: Any sample inclusion/exclusion rule, or null.
key_choices.data_processing: Any reshaping, recoding, aggregation, scaling, or transformation.
key_choices.model_choice: Main model or statistical test used.
key_choices.inference_rule: Rule used to judge support, e.g., p-value threshold, robust standard error, Bayesian rule, or null.

outcome.name: Name of the outcome variable or constructed outcome.
outcome.definition: How the outcome is defined or constructed from the data.

main_predictor.name: Name of the focal predictor or independent variable.
main_predictor.definition: How the focal predictor is defined or constructed from the data.

controls.name: Control variable name, if any.
controls.definition: Brief definition or construction rule for the control variable.

analysis_code.available: true if analysis code is available; false otherwise.
code_files: Files needed to execute this specific analysis task.
analysis_code.entry_file: Main script to run, if known.
analysis_code.run_command: Command to execute the analysis, if known.
analysis_code.notes: Task-specific notes about running the analysis code.

tasks.notes_for_agent: Additional instruction for the agent. Example: follow this path; do not change the focal claim, dataset, or analysis goal.

files.file_name: A detailed description of what this file does and how it relates to the analysis. Only include files relevant to execution.

codebase.notes: Any overall notes on the code design, dependencies, or runtime environment.

docker_specs.base_image: A proper base Docker image that contains the necessary software, e.g., python:3.10 or rocker/verse.

python: List of required Python packages with version constraints, if any.
r: List of required R packages with versions, if any.
other: Other necessary software, e.g., git, make, wget, Stata runtime, Matlab runtime.

hardware.gpu_support: true if GPU is required, false otherwise.
hardware.min_gpu_memory_gb: Minimum GPU memory required in GB, if applicable.
hardware.min_ram_gb: Minimum system RAM required in GB.

volumes: Suggested host paths to mount as volumes inside the container, e.g., ./data:/app/data.

analysis.instructions: High-level execution instruction for the agent. Example: execute the planned Task1 and Task2 paths using the original dataset, and produce structured execution outputs.
target_task_ids: List of task IDs the agent should execute, e.g., Task1 and Task2.
allowed_actions: Actions the agent may perform, e.g., inspect data files, inspect code files, run provided code, make bounded implementation fixes, write compact result extraction scripts.
not_allowed_actions: Actions the agent must not perform, e.g., change the focal claim, change the dataset, replace the planned path with a different analysis path, choose a more favorable result.

debugging_rule.max_repair_attempts: Maximum number of bounded repair attempts, e.g., 3.
debugging_rule.repair_scope: Allowed repair scope. Example: fix implementation, dependency, path, or output extraction issues only; do not redesign the analysis.

analysis.comparison_metrics: Metrics or outputs expected for later comparison, e.g., raw coefficient, p-value, test statistic, effect size, conclusion text.

executed_files: List of scripts/files actually executed by the agent.
raw_outputs: Files, logs, tables, console outputs, or extracted raw statistics that should be available after execution.
